DGE_WTvsdelta_37C_30C_thesis

Rachael Murray

2025-03-03

Summary

Differential Gene Expression WT vs delta at 37°C and WT vs delta at 30°C in YPD samples, using DESeq2

This analysis produces files:

  • deseq_df_WTvsdelta_37C_YPD.txt
  • deglist_WTvsdelta_37C_YPD_up2x_FDR5.txt
  • deglist_WTvsdelta_37C_YPD_down2x_FDR5.txt
  • deseq_df_WTvsdelta_30C_YPD.txt
  • deglist_WTvsdelta_30C_YPD_up2x_FDR5.txt
  • deglist_WTvsdelta_30C_YPD_down2x_FDR5.txt

This analysis produces figures:

  • WTvsDelta_37_YPD_volcano.png
  • WTvsDelta_30_YPD_volcano.png

Conclusions: - Significant differential gene expression between WT and delta samples at both 30°C and 37°C

  • Many genes up-regulated in flc1∆ are cell wall biosynthesis genes, membrane transporters, transcription factors

WT vs delta at 37°C

Load count data from shared script load_count_data.Rmd

Load and organise count data

load_count_data.Rmd is just to load and organise the raw count data in R, as well as loading libraries for data analysis. Other scripts can just run this one instead of duplicating code, especially scripts for differential gene expression analysis.

This runs after QC_PCA.Rmd, which does the organisation. This just loads the organised counts from the file results/summaries/counts_bysamplecode.txt.

Load the organised counts

counts_all <- 
  here::here("results", "summaries", "counts_bysamplecode.txt") %>%
  readr::read_tsv(comment = "#",
                  col_types = "ciiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiii")   %>%
  magrittr::set_rownames(.$Gene)
## Warning: Setting row names on a tibble is deprecated.
counts_all
## # A tibble: 6,795 × 32
##    Gene       Length WT_30_Y_1 WT_30_Y_2 WT_30_Y_3 FL_30_Y_1 FL_30_Y_2 FL_30_Y_3
##  * <chr>       <int>     <int>     <int>     <int>     <int>     <int>     <int>
##  1 CNAG_00002   1027       322       378       346       310       309       174
##  2 CNAG_00003   2397        19        43        27        54        52        33
##  3 CNAG_00004   3424        94        80        83       102        94        65
##  4 CNAG_00005   2162       462       399       385       378       344       310
##  5 CNAG_00006   1450      1019      1533      1256      1505      1359       778
##  6 CNAG_00007   2294       471       703       579       476       407       284
##  7 CNAG_00008   4351       141       226       206       272       259       164
##  8 CNAG_00009   1794       160       162       187       323       250       171
##  9 CNAG_00010   3879       195       199       242       362       272       159
## 10 CNAG_00011   2037        85       117       114       162       139        81
## # ℹ 6,785 more rows
## # ℹ 24 more variables: WT_37_Y_1 <int>, WT_37_Y_2 <int>, WT_37_Y_3 <int>,
## #   FL_37_Y_1 <int>, FL_37_Y_2 <int>, FL_37_Y_3 <int>, WT_37_C_1 <int>,
## #   WT_37_C_2 <int>, WT_37_C_3 <int>, FL_37_C_1 <int>, FL_37_C_2 <int>,
## #   FL_37_C_3 <int>, WT_37_E_1 <int>, WT_37_E_2 <int>, WT_37_E_3 <int>,
## #   FL_37_E_1 <int>, FL_37_E_2 <int>, FL_37_E_3 <int>, WT_37_CE_1 <int>,
## #   WT_37_CE_2 <int>, WT_37_CE_3 <int>, FL_37_CE_1 <int>, FL_37_CE_2 <int>, …

Load the sample sheet

sample_sheet <- 
  here::here("input", "experiment",
             "CryptoRNAseqFLC1SampleKey.csv") %>%
  readr::read_csv(comment = "#", col_types = "ccccccc") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Condition = stringr::str_sub(SampleName,start = 1L, end = -3L)) %>%
  dplyr::transmute(SampleID = as_factor(SampleID), 
                   SampleName = as_factor(SampleName),
                   Condition = as_factor(Condition),
                   FLC1 = factor(FLC1, levels = c("delta", "WT")),
                   Temperature = factor(Temperature, levels = c("30", "37")),
                   Media = as_factor(Media),
                   BioRep = as_factor(BioRep))

sample_sheet 
## # A tibble: 30 × 7
##    SampleID SampleName Condition FLC1  Temperature Media BioRep
##    <fct>    <fct>      <fct>     <fct> <fct>       <fct> <fct> 
##  1 1        WT_30_Y_1  WT_30_Y   WT    30          YPD   1     
##  2 2        WT_30_Y_2  WT_30_Y   WT    30          YPD   2     
##  3 3        WT_30_Y_3  WT_30_Y   WT    30          YPD   3     
##  4 4        FL_30_Y_1  FL_30_Y   delta 30          YPD   1     
##  5 5        FL_30_Y_2  FL_30_Y   delta 30          YPD   2     
##  6 6        FL_30_Y_3  FL_30_Y   delta 30          YPD   3     
##  7 7        WT_37_Y_1  WT_37_Y   WT    37          YPD   1     
##  8 8        WT_37_Y_2  WT_37_Y   WT    37          YPD   2     
##  9 9        WT_37_Y_3  WT_37_Y   WT    37          YPD   3     
## 10 10       FL_37_Y_1  FL_37_Y   delta 37          YPD   1     
## # ℹ 20 more rows

Load H99 Gene information

gene_info_df <- 
  here::here("input", "annotation",
             "H99_geneinfo_FungiDB59.txt") %>%
  readr::read_tsv(comment = "#")  %>% 
  dplyr::rename(gene = GeneID, geneName = GeneName)
## Rows: 8338 Columns: 6
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: "\t"
## chr (6): GeneID, ProductDescription, GeneName, CuratedGOComponents, CuratedG...
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.

DESeq on a subset of data, only 37C in YPD

sample_sheet_WTvsdelta_37C_YPD <-
  dplyr::filter(sample_sheet,
                Temperature == "37", Media == "YPD")

dds_WTvsdelta_37C_YPD <- 
  DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(
    countData = counts_all %>%
      dplyr::select(sample_sheet_WTvsdelta_37C_YPD$SampleName) %>%
      magrittr::set_rownames(counts_all$Gene),
    colData = sample_sheet_WTvsdelta_37C_YPD,
    design = ~ FLC1) %>%
  DESeq()
## Warning: Setting row names on a tibble is deprecated.
## estimating size factors
## estimating dispersions
## gene-wise dispersion estimates
## mean-dispersion relationship
## final dispersion estimates
## fitting model and testing
dds_WTvsdelta_37C_YPD
## class: DESeqDataSet 
## dim: 6795 6 
## metadata(1): version
## assays(4): counts mu H cooks
## rownames(6795): CNAG_00002 CNAG_00003 ... CNAG_14001 CNAG_14002
## rowData names(22): baseMean baseVar ... deviance maxCooks
## colnames(6): WT_37_Y_1 WT_37_Y_2 ... FL_37_Y_2 FL_37_Y_3
## colData names(8): SampleID SampleName ... BioRep sizeFactor
results(dds_WTvsdelta_37C_YPD)
## log2 fold change (MLE): FLC1 WT vs delta 
## Wald test p-value: FLC1 WT vs delta 
## DataFrame with 6795 rows and 6 columns
##             baseMean log2FoldChange     lfcSE       stat    pvalue      padj
##            <numeric>      <numeric> <numeric>  <numeric> <numeric> <numeric>
## CNAG_00002  445.3210     -0.0131039 0.1320603 -0.0992263 0.9209586  0.941903
## CNAG_00003   49.4729     -0.4829406 0.2730471 -1.7687077 0.0769427  0.128320
## CNAG_00004  107.5711      0.2423738 0.1890020  1.2823875 0.1997068  0.286034
## CNAG_00005  666.9180     -0.0198152 0.1245374 -0.1591104 0.8735819  0.909723
## CNAG_00006 1677.6572      0.1564381 0.0890361  1.7570196 0.0789145  0.131083
## ...              ...            ...       ...        ...       ...       ...
## CNAG_08027 333.77309     -0.3061507  0.132725  -2.306663 0.0210736  0.042386
## CNAG_08028 111.24722     -0.2633673  0.186173  -1.414638 0.1571747  0.234769
## CNAG_08029 107.44692      0.1728125  0.196800   0.878111 0.3798837  0.480572
## CNAG_14001 414.87927     -0.0445938  0.131145  -0.340033 0.7338315  0.799746
## CNAG_14002   1.24495     -1.1303981  1.635258  -0.691266 0.4893985  0.587557
resultsNames(dds_WTvsdelta_37C_YPD)
## [1] "Intercept"        "FLC1_WT_vs_delta"
deseq_df_WTvsdelta_37C_YPD <- 
  biobroom::tidy.DESeqDataSet(dds_WTvsdelta_37C_YPD) %>%
  dplyr::filter(term == "FLC1_WT_vs_delta") %>%
  dplyr::left_join(gene_info_df,
                   by = "gene") %>% 
  dplyr::select(gene, 
                geneName, 
                baseMean, 
                log2FC = estimate, 
                stderror,
                padj = p.adjusted)
## Warning: `tbl_df()` was deprecated in dplyr 1.0.0.
## ℹ Please use `tibble::as_tibble()` instead.
## ℹ The deprecated feature was likely used in the biobroom package.
##   Please report the issue at <https://github.com/StoreyLab/biobroom/issues>.
## This warning is displayed once every 8 hours.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.

List DEGs most UP in WT vs delta

We cut off at 2x changes with 5% FDR here.

# Make data frames of DEGs up and down, arranged by log2FC
DEGdf_WT_up2x_FDR5_37 <- 
  dplyr::filter(deseq_df_WTvsdelta_37C_YPD,
                log2FC > 1, padj < 0.05) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(log2FC))
print(DEGdf_WT_up2x_FDR5_37, n = 20)
## # A tibble: 40 × 6
##    gene       geneName baseMean log2FC stderror     padj
##    <chr>      <chr>       <dbl>  <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>

##  2 CNAG_03454 <NA>         65.7   1.94   0.618  4.61e- 3
##  3 CNAG_04469 <NA>         88.6   1.82   0.544  2.50e- 3
##  4 CNAG_07695 <NA>        123.    1.51   0.187  1.28e-14
##  5 CNAG_00987 <NA>        380.    1.39   0.133  8.95e-24

## 18 CNAG_01592 STE14        93.5   1.13   0.235  7.69e- 6

Print the top 20 named DEGs

DEGdf_WT_up2x_FDR5_37 %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(geneName)) %>%
  print(n = 20)
## # A tibble: 4 × 6
##   gene       geneName baseMean log2FC stderror     padj
##   <chr>      <chr>       <dbl>  <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>
## 1 CNAG_05144 CAN2       1327.    1.25    0.109 9.21e-29
## 2 CNAG_01592 STE14        93.5   1.13    0.235 7.69e- 6
## 3 CNAG_03664 NIC1        434.    1.12    0.127 2.91e-17
## 4 CNAG_02460 HEM13       230.    1.09    0.150 4.43e-12

List DEGs most DOWN in WT vs delta

# Make data frames of DEGs up and down, arranged by log2FC
DEGdf_WT_down2x_FDR5_37 <- 
  dplyr::filter(deseq_df_WTvsdelta_37C_YPD,
                log2FC < -1, padj < 0.05) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(log2FC)
print(DEGdf_WT_down2x_FDR5_37, n = 20)
## # A tibble: 610 × 6
##    gene       geneName baseMean log2FC stderror      padj
##    <chr>      <chr>       <dbl>  <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl>

## 16 CNAG_04735 MEP1      1672.    -4.07    0.131 1.33e-209

## # ℹ 590 more rows

Print the top 20 named DEGs

DEGdf_WT_down2x_FDR5_37 %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(geneName)) %>%
  print(n = 20)
## # A tibble: 97 × 6
##    gene       geneName baseMean log2FC stderror      padj
##    <chr>      <chr>       <dbl>  <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl>
##  1 CNAG_05662 PTP1        90.4   -4.81    0.399 1.06e- 31
##  2 CNAG_02217 CHS7       973.    -4.52    0.156 1.18e-182
##  3 CNAG_04735 MEP1      1672.    -4.07    0.131 1.33e-209

##  6 CNAG_01446 HSP12     6534.    -3.25    0.105 4.34e-206
##  7 CNAG_05377 ITR3        42.7   -3.23    0.405 2.62e- 14
##  8 CNAG_01562 BLP4     46184.    -3.10    0.107 4.95e-181
##  9 CNAG_06346 BLP1       149.    -2.83    0.221 7.53e- 36

## 17 CNAG_04016 ISP5       107.    -2.48    0.227 3.08e- 26

## # ℹ 77 more rows
plot_labels_down <- DEGdf_WT_down2x_FDR5_37 %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(geneName))

Volcano plot of DEGs

volcano_WTvsdelta_37C_YPD <- 
  ggplot(data = deseq_df_WTvsdelta_37C_YPD,
         aes(x = log2FC, y = -log10(padj)) ) +
  geom_point(size = 0.3, colour = "grey70") +
  geom_point(data = DEGdf_WT_up2x_FDR5_37,
             size = 0.3, colour = "darkblue") +
  geom_point(data = DEGdf_WT_down2x_FDR5_37,
             size = 0.3, colour = "darkred") +
  #geom_text_repel(data = plot_labels_down, aes(label = geneName), size = 3, colour = "darkred") + 
  scale_y_continuous("-log10(p)",
                     limits = c(0,50), expand = c(0,0),
                     oob=scales::squish) +
  scale_x_continuous("log2 fold-change\n← up in flc1∆       up in FLC1 →",
                     breaks = -6:6,
                     limits = c(-6,6), expand = c(0.01,0.01),
                     oob = scales::squish) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") 

volcano_WTvsdelta_37C_YPD 
## Warning: Removed 49 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_point()`).

#ggsave(here("WTvsDelta_37_YPD_volcano.png"), volcano_WTvsdelta_37C_YPD , width = 4, height = 3)

The volcano plot shows extensive differential gene expression, with more genes substantially down in WT (i.e. up in FLC1∆).

Write outputs to files

deseq_df_WTvsdelta_37C_YPD %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    across(c(baseMean, log2FC, stderror), round, digits = 3)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(padj = signif(padj, digits = 4)) %>%
  write_tsv(file = here::here("results", "DEGs",
                              "deseq_df_WTvsdelta_37C_YPD.txt"))
## Warning: There was 1 warning in `dplyr::mutate()`.
## ℹ In argument: `across(c(baseMean, log2FC, stderror), round, digits = 3)`.
## Caused by warning:
## ! The `...` argument of `across()` is deprecated as of dplyr 1.1.0.
## Supply arguments directly to `.fns` through an anonymous function instead.
## 
##   # Previously
##   across(a:b, mean, na.rm = TRUE)
## 
##   # Now
##   across(a:b, \(x) mean(x, na.rm = TRUE))
DEGdf_WT_up2x_FDR5_37 %>%
  dplyr::pull(gene) %>%
  write_lines(file = here::here("results", "DEGs",
                            "deglist_WTvsdelta_37C_YPD_up2x_FDR5.txt"))

DEGdf_WT_down2x_FDR5_37 %>%
  dplyr::pull(gene) %>%
  write_lines(file = here::here("results", "DEGs",
                            "deglist_WTvsdelta_37C_YPD_down2x_FDR5.txt"))

WT vs delta at 30°C

DESeq on a subset of data, only 30C in YPD

sample_sheet_WTvsdelta_30C_YPD <-
  dplyr::filter(sample_sheet,
                Temperature == "30", Media == "YPD")

dds_WTvsdelta_30C_YPD <- 
  DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(
    countData = counts_all %>%
      dplyr::select(sample_sheet_WTvsdelta_30C_YPD$SampleName) %>%
      magrittr::set_rownames(counts_all$Gene),
    colData = sample_sheet_WTvsdelta_30C_YPD,
    design = ~ FLC1) %>%
  DESeq()
## Warning: Setting row names on a tibble is deprecated.
## estimating size factors
## estimating dispersions
## gene-wise dispersion estimates
## mean-dispersion relationship
## final dispersion estimates
## fitting model and testing
dds_WTvsdelta_30C_YPD
## class: DESeqDataSet 
## dim: 6795 6 
## metadata(1): version
## assays(4): counts mu H cooks
## rownames(6795): CNAG_00002 CNAG_00003 ... CNAG_14001 CNAG_14002
## rowData names(22): baseMean baseVar ... deviance maxCooks
## colnames(6): WT_30_Y_1 WT_30_Y_2 ... FL_30_Y_2 FL_30_Y_3
## colData names(8): SampleID SampleName ... BioRep sizeFactor
results(dds_WTvsdelta_30C_YPD)
## log2 fold change (MLE): FLC1 WT vs delta 
## Wald test p-value: FLC1 WT vs delta 
## DataFrame with 6795 rows and 6 columns
##              baseMean log2FoldChange     lfcSE       stat      pvalue
##             <numeric>      <numeric> <numeric>  <numeric>   <numeric>
## CNAG_00002   304.2629       0.600187  0.163761   3.665012 0.000247327
## CNAG_00003    36.7405      -0.469196  0.336317  -1.395100 0.162985691
## CNAG_00004    85.9937       0.171813  0.240174   0.715368 0.474381446
## CNAG_00005   385.9369       0.421829  0.205504   2.052655 0.040106076
## CNAG_00006  1214.7328       0.263007  0.147334   1.785113 0.074242999
## ...               ...            ...       ...        ...         ...
## CNAG_08027 170.021816      -0.327187  0.192547 -1.6992550   0.0892712
## CNAG_08028  60.318903      -0.134385  0.289322 -0.4644806   0.6423035
## CNAG_08029  78.841107       0.146733  0.244746  0.5995330   0.5488175
## CNAG_14001 376.399451      -0.281509  0.172404 -1.6328487   0.1025008
## CNAG_14002   0.284371       0.156000  3.215062  0.0485215   0.9613007
##                  padj
##             <numeric>
## CNAG_00002 0.00107175
## CNAG_00003 0.26828297
## CNAG_00004 0.60524529
## CNAG_00005 0.08740082
## CNAG_00006 0.14391172
## ...               ...
## CNAG_08027   0.166709
## CNAG_08028   0.749338
## CNAG_08029   0.671098
## CNAG_14001   0.185798
## CNAG_14002   0.974720
resultsNames(dds_WTvsdelta_30C_YPD)
## [1] "Intercept"        "FLC1_WT_vs_delta"
deseq_df_WTvsdelta_30C_YPD <- 
  biobroom::tidy.DESeqDataSet(dds_WTvsdelta_30C_YPD) %>%
  dplyr::filter(term == "FLC1_WT_vs_delta") %>%
  dplyr::left_join(gene_info_df,
                   by = "gene") %>% 
  dplyr::select(gene, 
                geneName, 
                baseMean, 
                log2FC = estimate, 
                stderror,
                padj = p.adjusted)

List DEGs most UP in WT vs delta

We cut off at 2x changes with 5% FDR here.

# Make data frames of DEGs up and down, arranged by log2FC
DEGdf_WT_up2x_FDR5_30 <- 
  dplyr::filter(deseq_df_WTvsdelta_30C_YPD,
                log2FC > 1, padj < 0.05) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(log2FC))
print(DEGdf_WT_up2x_FDR5_30, n = 20)
## # A tibble: 201 × 6
##    gene       geneName baseMean log2FC stderror     padj
##    <chr>      <chr>       <dbl>  <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>

##  5 CNAG_06298 <NA>        52.8    1.93    0.371 1.75e- 6

##  8 CNAG_06745 H3       30828.     1.64    0.454 1.30e- 3
##  9 CNAG_00100 <NA>      4380.     1.63    0.231 3.01e-11
## 10 CNAG_07807 <NA>     17358.     1.57    0.517 7.88e- 3
## 11 CNAG_06746 H2B      49406.     1.57    0.490 4.93e- 3
## 12 CNAG_05340 <NA>        25.8    1.56    0.404 5.58e- 4
## 13 CNAG_06747 H2A-1    29224.     1.55    0.244 2.82e- 9
## 14 CNAG_02588 <NA>        19.1    1.54    0.476 4.26e- 3

## 16 CNAG_05059 <NA>      2266.     1.52    0.237 1.79e- 9
## 17 CNAG_06207 <NA>       266.     1.52    0.556 1.83e- 2
## 18 CNAG_04677 <NA>        73.4    1.49    0.297 4.09e- 6
## 19 CNAG_01153 <NA>     13909.     1.48    0.445 3.36e- 3
## 20 CNAG_00672 <NA>     40991.     1.47    0.205 1.60e-11
## # ℹ 181 more rows

Print the top 20 named DEGs

DEGdf_WT_up2x_FDR5_30 %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(geneName)) %>%
  print(n = 20)
## # A tibble: 31 × 6
##    gene       geneName   baseMean log2FC stderror     padj
##    <chr>      <chr>         <dbl>  <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>

##  3 CNAG_06746 H2B         49406.    1.57    0.490 4.93e- 3
##  4 CNAG_06747 H2A-1       29224.    1.55    0.244 2.82e- 9

## 17 CNAG_04758 AMT2           54.1   1.12    0.328 2.50e- 3
## 18 CNAG_01496 PPT1          275.    1.10    0.218 3.50e- 6

## 20 CNAG_00413 OFD1          697.    1.08    0.202 7.91e- 7
## # ℹ 11 more rows

List DEGs most DOWN in WT vs delta

# Make data frames of DEGs up and down, arranged by log2FC
DEGdf_WT_down2x_FDR5_30 <- 
  dplyr::filter(deseq_df_WTvsdelta_30C_YPD,
                log2FC < -1, padj < 0.05) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(log2FC)
print(DEGdf_WT_down2x_FDR5_30, n = 20)
## # A tibble: 733 × 6
##    gene       geneName baseMean log2FC stderror      padj
##    <chr>      <chr>       <dbl>  <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl>
##  1 CNAG_04891 <NA>      3787.    -9.04    0.273 2.57e-236
##  2 CNAG_01954 <NA>       200.    -8.58    0.858 6.93e- 22

##  5 CNAG_07447 <NA>        13.0   -6.08    2.50  3.82e-  2
##  6 CNAG_03867 <NA>        59.1   -6.06    0.690 5.55e- 17
##  7 CNAG_00588 <NA>       619.    -6.06    0.275 6.50e-105

## 13 CNAG_05662 PTP1       181.    -5.60    0.384 4.19e- 46

## 16 CNAG_03650 <NA>        79.8   -5.34    0.493 1.42e- 25
## 17 CNAG_00827 <NA>        36.4   -5.26    1.19  5.67e-  5
## 18 CNAG_01446 HSP12     2984.    -5.25    0.214 1.44e-129

Print the top 20 named DEGs

DEGdf_WT_down2x_FDR5_30 %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(geneName)) %>%
  print(n = 20)
## # A tibble: 120 × 6
##    gene       geneName baseMean log2FC stderror      padj
##    <chr>      <chr>       <dbl>  <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl>

Volcano plot of DEGs

volcano_WTvsdelta_30C_YPD <- 
  ggplot(data = deseq_df_WTvsdelta_30C_YPD,
         aes(x = log2FC, y = -log10(padj)) ) +
  geom_point(size = 0.3, colour = "grey70") +
  geom_point(data = DEGdf_WT_up2x_FDR5_30,
             size = 0.3, colour = "darkblue") +
  geom_point(data = DEGdf_WT_down2x_FDR5_30,
             size = 0.3, colour = "darkred") +
scale_y_continuous("-log10(p)",
                     limits = c(0,50), expand = c(0,0),
                     oob=scales::squish) +
  scale_x_continuous("log2 fold-change\n← up in flc1∆       up in FLC1 →",
                     breaks = -6:6,
                     limits = c(-6,6), expand = c(0.01,0.01),
                     oob = scales::squish) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") 

volcano_WTvsdelta_30C_YPD
## Warning: Removed 61 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_point()`).

ggsave(here("WTvsDelta_30_YPD_volcano.png"), volcano_WTvsdelta_30C_YPD , width = 4, height = 3)
## Warning: Removed 61 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_point()`).

The volcano plot shows extensive differential gene expression, with more genes substantially down in WT (i.e. up in FLC1∆).

Write outputs to files

deseq_df_WTvsdelta_30C_YPD %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    across(c(baseMean, log2FC, stderror), round, digits = 3)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(padj = signif(padj, digits = 4)) %>%
  write_tsv(file = here::here("results", "DEGs",
                              "deseq_df_WTvsdelta_30C_YPD.txt"))

DEGdf_WT_up2x_FDR5_30 %>%
  dplyr::pull(gene) %>%
  write_lines(file = here::here("results", "DEGs",
                            "deglist_WTvsdelta_30C_YPD_up2x_FDR5.txt"))

DEGdf_WT_down2x_FDR5_30 %>%
  dplyr::pull(gene) %>%
  write_lines(file = here::here("results", "DEGs",
                            "deglist_WTvsdelta_30C_YPD_down2x_FDR5.txt"))

Top DEG at 30°C and 37°C

FungiDBinfo_WT_down2x_FDR5_30 <- read_csv(
  here("results/DEGs_fungiDB_info/FungiDB_WTvsdelta_30C_YPD_down2x_FDR5.csv")
) %>% 
  janitor::clean_names() %>% 
  select(
    gene_id, product_description, interpro_id, interpro_description, curated_go_processes, curated_go_functions, curated_go_components, computed_go_processes, computed_go_functions, computed_go_components
  ) %>%
  dplyr::rename(gene = gene_id) %>% 
  left_join(
    DEGdf_WT_down2x_FDR5_30,
  )
## Rows: 733 Columns: 18
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## chr (15): Gene ID, Organism, Genomic Location (Gene), Product Description, G...
## dbl  (3): Input Order, Protein Length, CDS Length
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
## Joining with `by = join_by(gene)`
FungiDBinfo_WT_down2x_FDR5_30 %>% 
  slice_head(n = 20)
## # A tibble: 20 × 15
##    gene       product_description               interpro_id interpro_description
##    <chr>      <chr>                             <chr>       <chr>               
##  1 CNAG_04891 hypothetical protein              IPR000772;… Ricin B, lectin dom…
##  2 CNAG_01954 aldo-keto reductase               IPR023210;… NADP-dependent oxid…
##  3 CNAG_02526 hypothetical protein              N/A         N/A                 
##  4 CNAG_04142 tartrate transporter              IPR011701;… Major facilitator s…
##  5 CNAG_07447 hypothetical protein              N/A         N/A                 
##  6 CNAG_03867 hypothetical protein              N/A         N/A                 
##  7 CNAG_00588 hypothetical protein              IPR000772;… Ricin B, lectin dom…
##  8 CNAG_00269 Sorbitol dehydrogenase            IPR002328;… Alcohol dehydrogena…
##  9 CNAG_05075 solute carrier family 20 (sodium… IPR001204   Phosphate transport…
## 10 CNAG_01052 hypothetical protein              N/A         N/A                 
## 11 CNAG_03833 hypothetical protein              N/A         N/A                 
## 12 CNAG_01272 hypothetical protein              N/A         N/A                 
## 13 CNAG_05662 Polyol transporter protein 1      IPR003663;… Sugar/inositol tran…
## 14 CNAG_04553 hypothetical protein              IPR002347;… Short-chain dehydro…
## 15 CNAG_02217 Chitin synthase                   IPR004834;… Fungal chitin synth…
## 16 CNAG_03650 hypothetical protein              N/A         N/A                 
## 17 CNAG_00827 ribose 5-phosphate isomerase      IPR003500;… Sugar-phosphate iso…
## 18 CNAG_01446 heat shock protein, 12Kda         IPR007250   Heat shock protein …
## 19 CNAG_06347 pr4/barwin domain protein         IPR036908   RlpA-like domain su…
## 20 CNAG_05666 hypothetical protein              N/A         N/A                 
## # ℹ 11 more variables: curated_go_processes <chr>, curated_go_functions <chr>,
## #   curated_go_components <chr>, computed_go_processes <chr>,
## #   computed_go_functions <chr>, computed_go_components <chr>, geneName <chr>,
## #   baseMean <dbl>, log2FC <dbl>, stderror <dbl>, padj <dbl>
FungiDBinfo_WT_down2x_FDR5_37 <- read_csv(
  here("results/DEGs_fungiDB_info/FungiDB_WTvsdelta_37C_YPD_down2x_FDR5.csv")
) %>% 
  janitor::clean_names() %>% 
  select(
    gene_id, product_description, interpro_id, interpro_description, curated_go_processes, curated_go_functions, curated_go_components, computed_go_processes, computed_go_functions, computed_go_components
  ) %>%
  dplyr::rename(gene = gene_id) %>% 
  left_join(
    DEGdf_WT_down2x_FDR5_37,
  )
## Rows: 610 Columns: 18
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## chr (15): Gene ID, Organism, Genomic Location (Gene), Product Description, G...
## dbl  (3): Input Order, Protein Length, CDS Length
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
## Joining with `by = join_by(gene)`
FungiDBinfo_WT_down2x_FDR5_37 %>% 
  slice_head(n = 20)
## # A tibble: 20 × 15
##    gene       product_description               interpro_id interpro_description
##    <chr>      <chr>                             <chr>       <chr>               
##  1 CNAG_04891 hypothetical protein              IPR000772;… Ricin B, lectin dom…
##  2 CNAG_05075 solute carrier family 20 (sodium… IPR001204   Phosphate transport…
##  3 CNAG_02734 hypothetical protein              IPR036291   NAD(P)-binding doma…
##  4 CNAG_02526 hypothetical protein              N/A         N/A                 
##  5 CNAG_01272 hypothetical protein              N/A         N/A                 
##  6 CNAG_01960 efflux protein EncT               IPR011701;… Major facilitator s…
##  7 CNAG_05662 Polyol transporter protein 1      IPR003663;… Sugar/inositol tran…
##  8 CNAG_01121 hypothetical protein              N/A         N/A                 
##  9 CNAG_01954 aldo-keto reductase               IPR023210;… NADP-dependent oxid…
## 10 CNAG_02217 Chitin synthase                   IPR004834;… Fungal chitin synth…
## 11 CNAG_02934 hypothetical protein, variant, h… N/A         N/A                 
## 12 CNAG_00588 hypothetical protein              IPR000772;… Ricin B, lectin dom…
## 13 CNAG_01052 hypothetical protein              N/A         N/A                 
## 14 CNAG_01560 hypothetical protein              N/A         N/A                 
## 15 CNAG_03224 hypothetical protein              N/A         N/A                 
## 16 CNAG_04735 extracellular elastinolytic meta… IPR001842;… Peptidase M36, fung…
## 17 CNAG_00749 alternative sulfate transporter   IPR011701;… Major facilitator s…
## 18 CNAG_05916 hypothetical protein              N/A         N/A                 
## 19 CNAG_04142 tartrate transporter              IPR011701;… Major facilitator s…
## 20 CNAG_03782 hypothetical protein              N/A         N/A                 
## # ℹ 11 more variables: curated_go_processes <chr>, curated_go_functions <chr>,
## #   curated_go_components <chr>, computed_go_processes <chr>,
## #   computed_go_functions <chr>, computed_go_components <chr>, geneName <chr>,
## #   baseMean <dbl>, log2FC <dbl>, stderror <dbl>, padj <dbl>

Gene ontology analysis of DEG at 30°C and 37°C WT vs flc1∆

The following files were uploaded to fungiDB: - deglist_WTvsdelta_37C_YPD_up2x_FDR5.txt - deglist_WTvsdelta_37C_YPD_down2x_FDR5.txt - deglist_WTvsdelta_30C_YPD_up2x_FDR5.txt - deglist_WTvsdelta_30C_YPD_down2x_FDR5.txt

To help explain difference in transciptomes of WT and flc1∆ cells at 30°C and 37°C, I searched for gene ontology (GO) terms enriched in differentially expressed genes compared to all genes from C. neoformans var. grubii H99 (p-value cutoff = 0.05). I used fungiDB to look for curated and computed GO slim terms describing biological processes, molecular functions and cellular components.

# Open GO analysis for down regulated gene in WT vs flc1∆ at 30°C
MF_slim_30_down <- read_tsv(
  here("results/GOanalysis/GO_WTvsdelta_30C_YPD_down2x_FDR5_molecular_function_slim.tsv")
) %>% 
  mutate(
    Comparison = "WTvsdelta_30C_YPD",
    GO_type = "MF",
    DEG = "down"
  )
## Rows: 4 Columns: 11
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: "\t"
## chr (3): ID, Name, Result gene list
## dbl (8): Bgd count, Result count, Pct of bgd, Fold enrichment, Odds ratio, P...
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
BP_slim_30_down <- read_tsv(
  here("results/GOanalysis/GO_WTvsdelta_30C_YPD_down2x_FDR5_biological_process_slim.tsv")
) %>% 
  mutate(
    Comparison = "WTvsdelta_30C_YPD",
    GO_type = "BP",
    DEG = "down"
  )
## Rows: 2 Columns: 11
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: "\t"
## chr (3): ID, Name, Result gene list
## dbl (8): Bgd count, Result count, Pct of bgd, Fold enrichment, Odds ratio, P...
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
CC_slim_30_down <- read_tsv(
  here("results/GOanalysis/GO_WTvsdelta_30C_YPD_down2x_FDR5_cellular_component_slim.tsv")
) %>% 
  mutate(
    Comparison = "WTvsdelta_30C_YPD",
    GO_type = "CC",
    DEG = "down"
  )
## Rows: 4 Columns: 11
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: "\t"
## chr (3): ID, Name, Result gene list
## dbl (8): Bgd count, Result count, Pct of bgd, Fold enrichment, Odds ratio, P...
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
GO_slim_30_down <- bind_rows(
  MF_slim_30_down,
  BP_slim_30_down,
  CC_slim_30_down) %>%  
  janitor::clean_names() %>% 
  mutate(
    go_type = factor(go_type, levels = c("BP", "MF", "CC"), labels = c("Biological process", "Molecular function", "Cellular component"))
  ) %>% 
  group_by(go_type) %>% 
  arrange(desc(fold_enrichment), .by_group = T)

GO_slim_30_down$name <- factor(GO_slim_30_down$name, levels = rev(GO_slim_30_down$name))
  

# Open GO analysis for downregulated gene in WT vs flc1∆ at 37°C

MF_slim_37_down <- read_tsv(
  here("results/GOanalysis/GO_WTvsdelta_37C_YPD_down2x_FDR5_molecular_function_slim.tsv")
) %>% 
  mutate(
    Comparison = "WTvsdelta_37C_YPD",
    GO_type = "MF",
    DEG = "down"
  )
## Rows: 3 Columns: 11
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: "\t"
## chr (3): ID, Name, Result gene list
## dbl (8): Bgd count, Result count, Pct of bgd, Fold enrichment, Odds ratio, P...
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
BP_slim_37_down <- read_tsv(
  here("results/GOanalysis/GO_WTvsdelta_37C_YPD_down2x_FDR5_biological_process_slim.tsv")
) %>% 
  mutate(
    Comparison = "WTvsdelta_37C_YPD",
    GO_type = "BP",
    DEG = "down"
  )
## Rows: 2 Columns: 11
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: "\t"
## chr (3): ID, Name, Result gene list
## dbl (8): Bgd count, Result count, Pct of bgd, Fold enrichment, Odds ratio, P...
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
CC_slim_37_down <- read_tsv(
  here("results/GOanalysis/GO_WTvsdelta_37C_YPD_down2x_FDR5_cellular_component_slim.tsv")
) %>% 
  mutate(
    Comparison = "WTvsdelta_37C_YPD",
    GO_type = "CC",
    DEG = "down"
  )
## Rows: 4 Columns: 11
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: "\t"
## chr (3): ID, Name, Result gene list
## dbl (8): Bgd count, Result count, Pct of bgd, Fold enrichment, Odds ratio, P...
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
GO_slim_37_down <- bind_rows(
  MF_slim_37_down,
  BP_slim_37_down,
  CC_slim_37_down) %>%  
  janitor::clean_names() %>% 
  mutate(
    go_type = factor(go_type, levels = c("BP", "MF", "CC"), labels = c("Biological process", "Molecular function", "Cellular component"))
  ) %>% 
  group_by(go_type) %>% 
  arrange(desc(fold_enrichment), .by_group = T)

GO_slim_37_down$name <- factor(GO_slim_37_down$name, levels = rev(GO_slim_37_down$name))


# Plot GO fold enrichment for down at 30°C 
pGO_slim_30_down <- ggplot(
  data = GO_slim_30_down, 
  aes(x = 0, xend = fold_enrichment, y = name, colour = go_type, fill = go_type)
) + 
  geom_point(aes(x = fold_enrichment, y = name, size = result_count, fill = go_type)) +
  geom_segment() +
    labs(size="Genes", colour="GO term", fill = "GO term") + 
    xlab("Fold enrichment") + 
    ylab("") +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(0,2,4,6,8,10), limits = c(0,10), expand = c(0.01,0.01))

# Plot GO fold enrichment for down at 37°C 
pGO_slim_37_down <- ggplot(
  data = GO_slim_37_down, 
  aes(x = 0, xend = fold_enrichment, y = name, colour = go_type, fill = go_type)
) + 
  geom_point(aes(x = fold_enrichment, y = name, size = result_count, fill = go_type)) +
  geom_segment() +
    labs(size="Genes", colour="GO term", fill = "GO term") + 
    xlab("Fold enrichment") + 
    ylab("") +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(0,2,4,6,8,10), limits = c(0,10), expand = c(0.01,0.01))

# Plot side-by-side
plegend <- get_legend(
  pGO_slim_30_down + 
  theme(legend.box.margin = margin(2, 2, 2, 2),
        legend.justification = "top")
)
## Warning in get_plot_component(plot, "guide-box"): Multiple components found;
## returning the first one. To return all, use `return_all = TRUE`.
# add the legend to the row we made earlier. Give it one-third of 
# the width of one plot (via rel_widths).
plot_grid(
  pGO_slim_30_down + theme(legend.position="none") + ggtitle("Down in FLC1 vs flc1∆ in YPD at 30°C"),
  plegend,
  pGO_slim_37_down + theme(legend.position="none") + ggtitle("Down in FLC1 vs flc1∆ in YPD at 37°C"),
  nrow = 2,
  rel_widths = c(1, .4),
  align = "v")
## Warning: Graphs cannot be vertically aligned unless the axis parameter is set.
## Placing graphs unaligned.

# Open GO analysis for down regulated gene in WT vs flc1∆ at 30°C
MF_slim_30_up <- read_tsv(
  here("results/GOanalysis/GO_WTvsdelta_30C_YPD_up2x_FDR5_molecular_function_slim.tsv")
) %>% 
  mutate(
    Comparison = "WTvsdelta_30C_YPD",
    GO_type = "MF",
    DEG = "up"
  )
## Rows: 9 Columns: 11
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: "\t"
## chr (3): ID, Name, Result gene list
## dbl (8): Bgd count, Result count, Pct of bgd, Fold enrichment, Odds ratio, P...
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
BP_slim_30_up <- read_tsv(
  here("results/GOanalysis/GO_WTvsdelta_30C_YPD_up2x_FDR5_biological_process_slim.tsv")
) %>% 
  mutate(
    Comparison = "WTvsdelta_30C_YPD",
    GO_type = "BP", ,
    DEG = "up"
  )
## Rows: 2 Columns: 11
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: "\t"
## chr (3): ID, Name, Result gene list
## dbl (8): Bgd count, Result count, Pct of bgd, Fold enrichment, Odds ratio, P...
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
CC_slim_30_up <- read_tsv(
  here("results/GOanalysis/GO_WTvsdelta_30C_YPD_up2x_FDR5_cellular_component_slim.tsv")
) %>% 
  mutate(
    Comparison = "WTvsdelta_30C_YPD",
    GO_type = "CC", ,
    DEG = "up"
  )
## Rows: 7 Columns: 11
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: "\t"
## chr (3): ID, Name, Result gene list
## dbl (8): Bgd count, Result count, Pct of bgd, Fold enrichment, Odds ratio, P...
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
GO_slim_30_up <- bind_rows(
  MF_slim_30_up,
  BP_slim_30_up,
  CC_slim_30_up) %>%  
  janitor::clean_names() %>% 
  mutate(
    go_type = factor(go_type, levels = c("BP", "MF", "CC"), labels = c("Biological process", "Molecular function", "Cellular component"))
  ) %>% 
  group_by(go_type) %>% 
  arrange(desc(fold_enrichment), .by_group = T)

GO_slim_30_up$name <- factor(GO_slim_30_up$name, levels = rev(GO_slim_30_up$name))
  

# Open GO analysis for upregulated gene in WT vs flc1∆ at 37°C

MF_slim_37_up <- read_tsv(
  here("results/GOanalysis/GO_WTvsdelta_37C_YPD_up2x_FDR5_molecular_function_slim.tsv")
) %>% 
  mutate(
    Comparison = "WTvsdelta_37C_YPD",
    GO_type = "MF",
    DEG = "up"
  )
## Rows: 2 Columns: 11
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: "\t"
## chr (3): ID, Name, Result gene list
## dbl (8): Bgd count, Result count, Pct of bgd, Fold enrichment, Odds ratio, P...
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
BP_slim_37_up <- read_tsv(
  here("results/GOanalysis/GO_WTvsdelta_37C_YPD_up2x_FDR5_biological_process_slim.tsv")
) %>% 
  mutate(
    Comparison = "WTvsdelta_37C_YPD",
    GO_type = "BP",
    DEG = "up"
  )
## Rows: 1 Columns: 11
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: "\t"
## chr (3): ID, Name, Result gene list
## dbl (8): Bgd count, Result count, Pct of bgd, Fold enrichment, Odds ratio, P...
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
CC_slim_37_up <- read_tsv(
  here("results/GOanalysis/GO_WTvsdelta_37C_YPD_up2x_FDR5_cellular_component_slim.tsv")
) %>% 
  mutate(
    Comparison = "WTvsdelta_37C_YPD",
    GO_type = "CC",
    DEG = "up"
  )
## Rows: 1 Columns: 11
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: "\t"
## chr (3): ID, Name, Result gene list
## dbl (8): Bgd count, Result count, Pct of bgd, Fold enrichment, Odds ratio, P...
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
GO_slim_37_up <- bind_rows(
  MF_slim_37_up,
  BP_slim_37_up,
  CC_slim_37_up) %>%  
  janitor::clean_names() %>% 
  mutate(
    go_type = factor(go_type, levels = c("BP", "MF", "CC"), labels = c("Biological process", "Molecular function", "Cellular component"))
  ) %>% 
  group_by(go_type) %>% 
  arrange(desc(fold_enrichment), .by_group = T)

GO_slim_37_up$name <- factor(GO_slim_37_up$name, levels = rev(GO_slim_37_up$name))


# Plot GO fold enrichment for up at 30°C 
pGO_slim_30_up <- ggplot(
  data = GO_slim_30_up, 
  aes(x = 0, xend = fold_enrichment, y = name, colour = go_type, fill = go_type)
) + 
  geom_point(aes(x = fold_enrichment, y = name, size = result_count, fill = go_type)) +
  geom_segment() +
    labs(size="Genes", colour="GO term", fill = "GO term") + 
    xlab("Fold enrichment") + 
    ylab("") 
    #scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(0,2,4,6,8,10), limits = c(0,10), expand = c(0.01,0.01))

# Plot GO fold enrichment for up at 37°C 
pGO_slim_37_up <- ggplot(
  data = GO_slim_37_up, 
  aes(x = 0, xend = fold_enrichment, y = name, colour = go_type, fill = go_type)
) + 
  geom_point(aes(x = fold_enrichment, y = name, size = result_count, fill = go_type)) +
  geom_segment() +
    labs(size="Genes", colour="GO term", fill = "GO term") + 
    xlab("Fold enrichment") + 
    ylab("") 
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(0,2,4,6,8,10), limits = c(0,10), expand = c(0.01,0.01))
## <ScaleContinuousPosition>
##  Range:  
##  Limits:    0 --   10
# Plot side-by-side
plegend <- get_legend(
  pGO_slim_30_up + 
  theme(legend.box.margin = margin(2, 2, 2, 2),
        legend.justification = "top")
)
## Warning in get_plot_component(plot, "guide-box"): Multiple components found;
## returning the first one. To return all, use `return_all = TRUE`.
# add the legend to the row we made earlier. Give it one-third of 
# the width of one plot (via rel_widths).
plot_grid(
  pGO_slim_30_up + theme(legend.position="none") + ggtitle("Up in FLC1 vs flc1∆ in YPD at 30°C"),
  plegend,
  pGO_slim_37_up + theme(legend.position="none") + ggtitle("Up in FLC1 vs flc1∆ in YPD at 37°C"),
  nrow = 2,
  rel_widths = c(1, .4),
  align = "v")
## Warning: Graphs cannot be vertically aligned unless the axis parameter is set.
## Placing graphs unaligned.

GOslim <- bind_rows(GO_slim_37_up, GO_slim_30_up, GO_slim_37_down, GO_slim_30_down) %>% 
  mutate(comparison = factor(comparison, 
                             levels = c("WTvsdelta_30C_YPD", "WTvsdelta_37C_YPD"),
                             labels = c("FLC1 vs flc1∆ at 30°C", "FLC1 vs flc1∆ at 37°C")),
         deg = factor(deg, levels = c("down", "up"), labels = c("up in flc1∆", "up in FLC1"))) %>% 
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(go_type, comparison) %>% 
  arrange(desc(fold_enrichment), .by_group = T) %>% 
  filter(!name %in% c("cellular component", "molecular function", "biological process", "organelle", "intracellular anatomical structure"))

GOslim$name <- factor(GOslim$name, levels = rev(unique(GOslim$name)))

NS_dataframe <- tibble(
name = c("rRNA binding", "unfolded protein binding", "RNA binding", "ligase activity", "acyltransferase activity", "oxidoreductase activity", "extracellular region", "cytosol", "mitochondrion", "lyase activity"),
comparison = c(rep("FLC1 vs flc1∆ at 37°C", times = 10)),
deg = c(rep("up in FLC1", times = 9), "up in flc1∆")
) %>% 
  mutate(deg = factor(deg, levels = c("up in flc1∆", "up in FLC1")))

# Plot GO temrs without filling in 'NA' to make left and right plots match  
#ggplot(
 # data = GOslim) + 
  #geom_segment(aes(x = 0, xend = fold_enrichment, y = name, linetype = go_type)) +
  #scale_linetype_manual(values = c(1,2,3)) + 
  #ggnewscale::new_scale_color() +  
  #geom_point(aes(x = fold_enrichment, y = name, size = result_count, colour = deg)) +
  #scale_colour_manual(values = c("darkred", "darkblue")) + 
   # labs(size="Number of Genes", linetype="GO term", colour = "Gene Expression") + 
  #  xlab("Fold enrichment") + 
   # ylab("") + 
    #scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(0,2,4,6,8,10, 12, 14), limits = c(0,14), expand = c(0.01,0.01)) +
    #facet_wrap2(deg~comparison, scales = "free_y", axes = "all") + 
    #theme(strip.text = element_text(colour = "white"),
     #     axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10, colour = "black"),
      #    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10, colour = "black"),
       #   axis.title.x = element_text(size = 12, colour = "black"),
        #  legend.title = element_text(size = 12, colour = "black"),
         # legend.text = element_text(size = 10, colour = "black"),
          #legend.justification = "top",
          #legend.spacing.y = unit(1.0, 'cm'),
          #legend.box.margin = margin(t = 0, r = 0, b = 0, l = 1, unit = 'cm')) + 
 # guides(linetype = guide_legend(order=1),
  #       colour = guide_legend(order=2),
   #      size = guide_legend(order=3))

#ggsave(here("GOanalysis_WTvsDelta_YPD_30C_37C.png"), height = 5, width = 12, dpi = 1000, bg = "white")
# In this plot, GO term fold enrichment is plotted on the X axis and GO terms are plotted on the y axis. GO term type is indicated using different lines (solid, dashed, dotted) and number of genes per GO term is inficated using point size. Blue indicated GO termns up in FLC1 and red indicates GO termns up in flc1∆. 
GO_slim_plot_WTvsdetla <- ggplot(
  data = GOslim) + 
  geom_segment(aes(x = 0, xend = fold_enrichment, y = name, linetype = go_type)) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c(1,2,3)) + 
  ggnewscale::new_scale_color() +  
  geom_point(aes(x = fold_enrichment, y = name, size = result_count, colour = deg)) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("darkred", "darkblue")) + 
    labs(size="Number of Genes", linetype="GO term", colour = "Gene Expression") + 
    xlab("Fold enrichment") + 
    ylab("") + 
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(0,2,4,6,8,10, 12, 14), limits = c(0,14), expand = c(0.01,0.01)) +
    #ggforce::facet_col(deg~comparison, scales = "free_y", space = "free") + 
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 3))

# Add in 'NA' symbols to make left and right plots symmertcial. The ggh4x package has a nice facet function that allows you to display the axes of all plots
GO_slim_plot_WTvsdetla + 
  geom_text(data = NS_dataframe, aes(x = 0.8, y = name, label = "NA"), size = 3) + 
    facet_grid2(deg~comparison, scale = "free_y", space = "free",  axes = "all") + 
    theme(strip.text.y = element_blank(),
          strip.text.x = element_text(size = 16, colour = "black", family="Arial"),
          axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14, colour = "black", family="Arial"),
          axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14, colour = "black", family="Arial"),
          axis.title.x = element_text(size = 16, colour = "black", family="Arial"),
          legend.title = element_text(size = 16, colour = "black", family="Arial"),
          legend.text = element_text(size = 14, colour = "black", family="Arial"),
          panel.spacing.x = unit(4, "lines"),
          legend.justification = "top",
          legend.spacing.y = unit(1.0, 'cm'),
          legend.box.margin = margin(t = -0.5, r = 0, b = 0, l = 2, unit = 'cm')
          ) + 
  guides(linetype = guide_legend(order=1),
         colour = guide_legend(order=2),
         size = guide_legend(order=3))

#ggsave(here("GOanalysis_WTvsDelta_YPD_30C_37C_grid.png"), height = 8, width = 16, dpi = 1000, bg = "white")
GOslim_WTvsdelat_YPD_30 <- ggplot(
  data = GOslim %>% filter(comparison == "FLC1 vs flc1∆ at 30°C")) + 
  geom_segment(aes(x = 0, xend = fold_enrichment, y = name, linetype = go_type)) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c("solid", "twodash", "dotted")) + 
  ggnewscale::new_scale_color() +  
  geom_point(aes(x = fold_enrichment, y = name, size = result_count, colour = deg)) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("darkred", "darkblue")) + 
    labs(size="Number of Genes", linetype="GO term", colour = "Gene Expression") + 
    xlab("Fold enrichment") + 
    ylab("") + 
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(0,2,4,6,8,10, 12, 14), limits = c(0,14), expand = c(0.01,0.01)) +
    facet_grid2(deg~., scales = "free_y", axes = "all", space = "free") + 
    theme(strip.text = element_text(colour = "white"),
          axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10, colour = "black"),
          axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10, colour = "black"),
          axis.title.x = element_text(size = 12, colour = "black"),
          legend.title = element_text(size = 12, colour = "black"),
          legend.text = element_text(size = 10, colour = "black"),
          legend.justification = "top",
          legend.spacing.y = unit(0.3, 'cm'),
          legend.box.margin = margin(t = 0, r = 0, b = 0, l = 1, unit = 'cm')) + 
  guides(linetype = guide_legend(order=1),
         colour = guide_legend(order=2),
         size = guide_legend(order=3))

#ggsave(here("GOanalysis_37Cvs30C_WT_vertical.png"), height = 5, width = 7, dpi = 1000, bg = "white")


Volcano_GOterm_30 <- cowplot::plot_grid(volcano_WTvsdelta_30C_YPD, GOslim_WTvsdelat_YPD_30, rel_widths = c(1:4),
                                        labels = c('A', 'B'))
## Warning: Removed 61 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_point()`).
Volcano_GOterm_30

#ggsave(here("results/figures/WTvsdelta_YPD_30_volcano_GOterm.png"),Volcano_GOterm_30, height = 4.5, width = 12, dpi = 1000, bg = "white")
GOslim_WTvsdelat_YPD_37 <- ggplot(
  data = GOslim %>% filter(comparison == "FLC1 vs flc1∆ at 37°C")) + 
  geom_segment(aes(x = 0, xend = fold_enrichment, y = name, linetype = go_type)) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c("solid", "twodash", "dotted")) + 
  ggnewscale::new_scale_color() +  
  geom_point(aes(x = fold_enrichment, y = name, size = result_count, colour = deg)) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("darkred", "darkblue")) + 
    labs(size="Number of Genes", linetype="GO term", colour = "Gene Expression") + 
    xlab("Fold enrichment") + 
    ylab("") + 
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(0,2,4,6,8,10, 12, 14), limits = c(0,14), expand = c(0.01,0.01)) +
    facet_grid2(deg~., scales = "free_y", axes = "all", space = "free") + 
    theme(strip.text = element_text(colour = "white"),
          axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10, colour = "black"),
          axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10, colour = "black"),
          axis.title.x = element_text(size = 12, colour = "black"),
          legend.title = element_text(size = 12, colour = "black"),
          legend.text = element_text(size = 10, colour = "black"),
          legend.justification = "top",
          legend.spacing.y = unit(0.3, 'cm'),
          legend.box.margin = margin(t = 0, r = 0, b = 0, l = 1, unit = 'cm')) + 
  guides(linetype = guide_legend(order=1),
         colour = guide_legend(order=2),
         size = guide_legend(order=3))

#ggsave(here("GOanalysis_37Cvs37C_WT_vertical.png"), height = 5, width = 7, dpi = 1000, bg = "white")


Volcano_GOterm_37 <- cowplot::plot_grid(volcano_WTvsdelta_37C_YPD, GOslim_WTvsdelat_YPD_37, rel_widths = c(1:4), labels = c('C', 'D'))
## Warning: Removed 49 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_point()`).
Volcano_GOterm_37 

#ggsave(here("results/figures/WTvsdelta_YPD_37_volcano_GOterm.png"),Volcano_GOterm_37, height = 4.5, width = 12, dpi = 1000, bg = "white")

Create a list of genes to highlight on volcano plots

Ca2+ channels and pumps: - CNAG_01232 = PMC1 (Calcium pump) - CNAG_01704 = McSc channel - CNAG_06060 = Ca2+ dependant channel (Csc1 family) - CNAG_07538 = Ca2+/Na+ exchanger (IPR004837 family) - CNAG_01208 = CCH1 - CNAG_00025 = VCX1 - CNAG_00107 = MCU family - CNAG_03805 = VCX1

Cell wall synthesis genes: - CNAG_01230 = chitin deacetylase 2 - CNAG_06501 = GAS1 - CNAG_05818 = CHS5 - CNAG_02217 = CHS7 - CNAG_06508 = FKS1 - CNAG_07499 = CHS8 - CNAG_02225 = EXG104 - CNAG_05799 = CDA1 (chitin deacetylase 1) - CNAG_06031 = KRE63 - CNAG_06835 = KRE61 - CNAG_00546 = CHS4 - CNAG_06726 = CSR3 - CNAG_06336 = BGL2 - CNAG_06291 = polysaccharide deacetylase (Cda4/Fpd1) - CNAG_00596 = UTR2 - CNAG_03412 = Chitinase

FLC1 and FLC2: - CNAG_00066 = FLC2 - CNAG_04283 = FLC1

Other interesing proteins: - CNAG_00979 = CTR4 - CNAG_00036 = SEC14-2 - CNAG_01446 = HSP12 - CNAG_03385 = PCL103 (cyclin) - CNAG_01562 = BLP4 - CNAG_02008 = OVA1 - CNAG_03464 = LAC2 - CNAG_03465 = LAC1 - CNAG_06347 = BLP2 - CNAG_06346 = BLP1 - CNAG_07701 = CTR1

Venn diagrams of up and down regulated genes WT vs delta at 37°C and 30°C

# Turn into lists of genes
DGE_30 <- bind_rows(DEGdf_WT_down2x_FDR5_30, DEGdf_WT_up2x_FDR5_30) %>%
.$gene
DGE_37 <- bind_rows(DEGdf_WT_down2x_FDR5_37, DEGdf_WT_up2x_FDR5_37) %>%
.$gene 

# Add to a list object with names to display on plot
euler_list <- list( 
  '30°C' = DGE_30, 
  '37°C' = DGE_37)

# Create the euler plot
eulerr_plot <- plot(
  euler(euler_list), 
  quantities = TRUE, 
  legend = TRUE,
  fills = c("grey95", "#F9E9C8"),
  fontsize = 20
  ) 

# Display eulerr 
eulerr_plot

DEGdf_WT_down2x_FDR5_37 %>% filter(!gene %in% DEGdf_WT_down2x_FDR5_30$gene)
## # A tibble: 153 × 6
##    gene       geneName baseMean log2FC stderror      padj
##    <chr>      <chr>       <dbl>  <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl>
##  1 CNAG_01560 <NA>         3.89  -4.13    1.40  0.00791  
##  2 CNAG_03797 <NA>        12.6   -3.57    0.760 0.0000132
##  3 CNAG_05278 <NA>         2.45  -3.43    1.51  0.0460   
##  4 CNAG_07793 <NA>         4.55  -3.39    1.23  0.0139   
##  5 CNAG_06911 <NA>         5.82  -3.16    1.03  0.00584  
##  6 CNAG_05324 <NA>         5.02  -2.88    1.05  0.0138   
##  7 CNAG_02489 <NA>        13.0   -2.87    0.652 0.0000456
##  8 CNAG_03551 <NA>         5.00  -2.87    1.07  0.0166   
##  9 CNAG_02521 <NA>         4.54  -2.75    1.08  0.0240   
## 10 CNAG_03842 <NA>         7.91  -2.58    0.829 0.00501  
## # ℹ 143 more rows

Look for transcription factors in DEG at 30°C and 37°C

# Read list of putative C.neoformans TFs from Jung et al. 2015
TFs <- read_csv(here("TFlist.csv")) %>% 
  mutate(gene = paste("CNAG_", gene, sep = ""))
## Rows: 178 Columns: 1
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## chr (1): gene
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
# Filter for TFs in DEGs
DEGdf_WT_down2x_FDR5_37 %>% 
  filter(gene %in% TFs$gene) 
## # A tibble: 16 × 6
##    gene       geneName baseMean log2FC stderror     padj
##    <chr>      <chr>       <dbl>  <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>
##  1 CNAG_04837 MLN1         10.2  -2.23    0.680 3.05e- 3

  filter(gene %in% TFs$gene)
